skill.name: things-mac
skill.description: Manage Things 3 via the `things` CLI on macOS (add/update projects+todos via URL scheme; read/search/list from the local Things database). Use when a user asks OpenClaw to add a task to Things, list inbox/today/upcoming, search tasks, or inspect projects/areas/tags.
skill.summary: Control Things 3 task manager on macOS using the command-line interface for reading, searching, and updating tasks.
skill.author: mylukin

security_audit.summary: All 60 static findings evaluated as false positives. The skill is a legitimate macOS task management tool using the official things3-cli. The detected patterns are documentation examples showing CLI commands (not executable code), a GitHub homepage reference to the open-source tool, and legitimate database read operations. No malicious intent detected.

medium_findings.title: External Command References in Documentation
medium_findings.description: Documentation contains 49 examples of `things` CLI commands with shell syntax patterns. These are documentation examples showing usage patterns, not executable code. Commands use hardcoded strings for the things3-cli tool with no user input injection vectors.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: All command examples are hardcoded strings for the things3-cli tool. No shell injection risk as the CLI tool handles its own argument parsing and the skill does not construct commands from user input.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded GitHub URL Reference
low_findings.description: Line 4 contains a reference to the things3-cli GitHub homepage. This is a legitimate open-source tool reference, not credential exfiltration or malicious network activity.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URL points to the official things3-cli project homepage. No network requests are made by the skill itself.

low_findings.title: Database Read Operations
low_findings.description: Lines 35, 70-76 show search and list operations against the local Things database. These are standard task management read operations, not system reconnaissance.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The operations are reading task lists and search results from a local task manager database, not scanning system resources or network interfaces.

content.value_statement: This skill connects Claude to Things 3, enabling natural language task management. Add tasks during conversations, search your projects, and update todos without leaving your workflow.
actual_capabilities: Add new tasks to Things 3 inbox with title, notes, deadline, and tags
Search tasks by keyword and retrieve matching items with IDs
List inbox, today, and upcoming tasks from Things 3
Update existing task titles, notes, tags, and completion status
Move tasks between projects and areas
Query projects, areas, and tags from the Things 3 database
limitations: macOS only (requires Things 3 app and things CLI)
Delete/trash operations not supported by things3-cli
Requires Full Disk Access for database reads in some configurations

use_cases.title: Add tasks during conversations
use_cases.description: Quickly create tasks from chat without switching apps. Say 'add buy groceries to tonight' and it appears in Things.
use_cases.target_user: Productivity-focused users who want to capture tasks without leaving conversations

use_cases.title: Search and retrieve task IDs
use_cases.description: Find existing tasks by name and get their UUIDs for follow-up operations like completing or updating.
use_cases.target_user: Users managing existing projects who need to reference or modify specific tasks

use_cases.title: Bulk task updates
use_cases.description: Update multiple task properties including title, notes, tags, project assignment, and completion status using IDs.
use_cases.target_user: Power users who organize tasks programmatically or clean up project boards

prompt_templates.title: Add a simple task
prompt_templates.prompt: Add "{task title}" to my Things inbox

prompt_templates.title: Add task with details
prompt_templates.prompt: Add "{task title}" to Things with notes "{notes}" and deadline {date}

prompt_templates.title: Search for tasks
prompt_templates.prompt: Search Things for "{query}" and show me the results

prompt_templates.title: List daily tasks
prompt_templates.prompt: Show me my Things today view and inbox items

output_examples.input: Add "Review project proposal" to Things with notes "Check budget section" and deadline tomorrow
output_examples.output:
Task added to Things 3 inbox: 'Review project proposal'
- Notes: Check budget section
- Deadline: Tomorrow
- Status: Pending

Found 2 matching tasks:
1. [Pending] Draft quarterly report - Project: Finance
2. [Pending] Review quarterly metrics - Project: Analytics

output_examples.input: Show me what's on my Things today list
output_examples.output:
Today's Tasks:
- Review project proposal (Due today)
- Send follow-up email (Due today)
- Team standup prep (Anytime)

best_practices: Use --dry-run flag first to preview URL scheme actions before execution
Store THINGS_AUTH_TOKEN in environment variables for authenticated updates
Grant Full Disk Access to your calling app for reliable database reads
anti_patterns: Do not use without things3-cli installed (requires 'things' binary in PATH)
Do not pass unvalidated user input directly to CLI arguments
Do not attempt delete operations (not supported by things3-cli)

faq.question: How do I install the things CLI?
Why are database reads failing?
faq.answer: Grant Full Disk Access to the calling app (Terminal or OpenClaw.app) in System Settings > Privacy & Security.

faq.question: How do I update existing tasks?
faq.answer: First search for the task to get its UUID, then use things update --id <UUID> with your changes.

faq.question: Can I delete tasks?
faq.answer: No, things3-cli does not support delete. Use things update --id <UUID> --completed or --canceled instead.

faq.question: What is the auth token for?
faq.answer: The THINGS_AUTH_TOKEN enables update operations. Set it as an environment variable to avoid passing --auth-token each time.

faq.question: Is this skill available on Windows or Linux?
faq.answer: No, Things 3 is macOS-only and this skill requires the things3-cli which is designed for macOS.
